metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Develop foundational programming and computational thinking skills through hands-on coding in Python or similar languages. Learn how to break down complex problems into manageable steps, design algorithms, and implement solutions. Explore fundamental data structures and discover how mathematical thinking underlies all computer science.
metadata.general_description: Learn programming fundamentals and computational thinking through practical problem-solving.

big_ideas.statement: Computational thinking is a problem-solving approach applicable across all disciplines and is fundamental to computer science.
big_ideas.elaboration: Breaking problems into smaller parts, recognizing patterns, abstracting details, and designing step-by-step solutions are processes used by programmers and mathematicians alike.

big_ideas.statement: Algorithms are precise sequences of instructions that can be executed by computers to solve problems reliably and efficiently.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding how to design, analyze, and optimize algorithms is central to programming. The same algorithm implemented in different languages shares the same logical structure.

big_ideas.statement: Data structures organize information in ways that make programs efficient and solutions elegant.
big_ideas.elaboration: Choosing appropriate structures like lists, dictionaries, and sets determines how effectively a program can manipulate and access data. Different problems call for different organizational approaches.

big_ideas.statement: Programming is a tool for expressing mathematical and logical ideas with precision and testing them computationally.
big_ideas.elaboration: Code translates mathematical concepts into executable instructions. Programs serve as laboratories for testing mathematical hypotheses and exploring complex systems.

categories.name: Reasoning and Modelling
categories.raw: Develop algorithmic thinking and logical reasoning through the design and analysis of computational solutions.

competencies.raw: Analyze problems and decompose them into manageable computational tasks using algorithmic thinking.
i_can_statements: I can break down complex problems into simpler subproblems and identify the sequence of steps needed to solve them.
I can recognize patterns in problems and apply them to design efficient algorithms.
I can trace through algorithms step-by-step to verify their correctness.
I can analyze the efficiency of different algorithmic approaches and choose appropriate ones for various problems.

categories.name: Understanding and Solving
categories.raw: Understand programming concepts and implement solutions using appropriate data structures and control flow.

competencies.raw: Implement algorithms in a programming language, using variables, data types, control structures, and functions effectively.
i_can_statements: I can write syntactically correct code using variables, data types, and operations appropriate to the problem.
I can implement sequences, loops, and conditional statements to control program flow.
I can create functions to organize code and solve subproblems within larger programs.
I can work with and manipulate fundamental data structures like lists, strings, and dictionaries.

categories.name: Communicating and Representing
categories.raw: Express computational ideas and program logic through code, comments, and visual representations.

competencies.raw: Communicate program logic and design through clear code, documentation, and visual representations like flowcharts.
i_can_statements: I can write code with meaningful variable names and comments that explain my logic.
I can create flowcharts or pseudocode to communicate algorithm design before writing actual code.
I can explain how my program works to others in both technical and non-technical language.
I can read and interpret code written by others and understand its purpose and logic.

categories.name: Connecting and Reflecting
categories.raw: Connect programming to other disciplines and reflect on how computational thinking applies broadly.

competencies.raw: Reflect on the applications of computational thinking across disciplines and the broader impact of technology.
i_can_statements: I can identify computational thinking approaches in non-programming contexts.
I can explain how mathematics underlies programming and algorithms.
I can recognize how computer science connects to other subjects like science, business, and social studies.
I can reflect on the ethical considerations of creating software and the responsibilities of programmers.

areas.name: Programming Fundamentals
areas.raw: Learn the basic building blocks of programming including syntax, data types, and program structure.
topics: Variables and data types (integers, floats, strings, booleans)

areas.name: Control Flow and Logic
areas.raw: Master the techniques for directing program execution based on conditions and repetition.

areas.name: Functions and Modularity
areas.raw: Organize code into reusable functions to solve complex problems systematically.
topics: Functions as tools for code reuse

areas.name: Data Structures
areas.raw: Work with collections of data using lists, dictionaries, sets, and other structures.

areas.name: Algorithms and Problem-Solving
areas.raw: Design and implement algorithms to solve increasingly complex problems efficiently.
